name: file-analyzer
description: Analyze files for complexity, structure, and quality metrics
usage: Add files, optionally set analysis depth and output format, then copy

depthInstructions.quick: Provide a high-level overview only. Focus on: file purpose, main exports/entry points, and any obvious red flags. Keep each file analysis to 3-5 bullet points.
depthInstructions.standard: Provide a thorough analysis covering: file purpose, structure and organization, key functions/classes, complexity assessment, dependency analysis, and improvement suggestions.
depthInstructions.deep: Provide an exhaustive analysis including: file purpose and design rationale, complete structure breakdown, cyclomatic complexity estimates per function, coupling analysis, SOLID principle adherence, design pattern identification, test coverage implications, refactoring opportunities with effort estimates, and security surface area.

formatInstructions.prose: Present the analysis as narrative prose with section headers. Use paragraphs for explanations and inline code references.
formatInstructions.structured: Present the analysis in a structured format with: a metrics summary table, bullet-point findings organized by category, and a scored report card (A-F) for each quality dimension.
formatInstructions.json: Present the analysis as a JSON object with keys: {fileName, purpose, metrics: {complexity, coupling, cohesion}, findings: [{category, severity, description}], score, suggestions: [string]}.

usageTemplate:
analyze            Run analysis with current settings
  set-depth <level>  Set depth: quick, standard, deep
  set-format <fmt>   Set output: prose, structured, json

commands.name: analyze
commands.description: Show current analysis settings and preview

flagDefs.name: depth
flagDefs.description: Analysis depth: quick, standard, or deep

flagDefs.name: format
flagDefs.description: Output format: prose, structured, or json

commands.name: set-depth
set-format
